chunks.text: THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD Criminal Bail Application No.S-1225 of 2024 Criminal Bail Application No.S-1301 of 2024 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 03.01.2025 Mr. Jahanzaib @ Jansher Memon, advocate for applicant in Criminal Bail Application No.S-1225/2024. Mr. Ghuluam Farooque Abbasi and Ms. Fareeda Naz advocates for applicant in Criminal Bail Application No.S-1301/2024. Mr. Irfan Ali Talpur, Assistant Prosecutor General, Sindh. ASI Ghulam Abbas Laghari, PS Tando Muhammad Khan. ASI Ghulam Shabbir Abro, PS Tando Muhammad Khan. Applicants are present on ad-interim pre-arrest bail. O R D E R Mr. Jahanzaib @ Jansher Memon, advocate files power on behalf of complainant in Criminal Bail Application No.S-1301/2024, whereas Mr. Ghuluam Farooque Abbasi, advocate files power on behalf of complainant in Criminal Bail Application No.S-1225/2024, which are taken on record. The cases are counter-cases registered by the parties against each other. Today, both parties have filed affidavits stating that they have resolved their disputes amicably and intend to approach an MPA, namely Muhammad Saleh Halepoto, to finalize the settlement outlines. Both parties have expressed no objection to confirmation of bail. In view of compromise between the parties and no objection by each counsel against applicants/accused, learned APG has also given no objection. The offences with which the applicants/accused are charged are compoundable. Therefore, the affidavits filed by the complainants in both cases are taken on record. In light of these affidavits and no-objection of the complainants, these applications are allowed. Consequently, the ad- interim pre-arrest bail granted to the applicants vide orders dated

In light of these affidavits and no-objection of the complainants, these applications are allowed. Consequently, the ad- interim pre-arrest bail granted to the applicants vide orders dated 2 11.11.2024 and 04.12.2024 is hereby confirmed on the same terms and conditions. However, if the parties fail to finalize the settlement before learned trial court, the other aggrieved party, including the prosecution, shall be at liberty to file an application seeking a revisit of this order. Both the applications are accordingly disposed of. JUDGE Irfan Ali
